name: Chauncey Morehouse
abstract: Chauncey Morehouse (March 11, 1902 – October 31, 1980) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Benefit: American Committee for Relief of German Children
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Bix Beiderbecke

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: he had Chauncey Morehouse and uh Charlie Horvath is that how you pronounce it Horvath the uh what was the arranger no Horvath was a drummer yeah who done the writing I don't know to tell you the truth sometimes Russ Morgan that's him yeah Russ Morgan did most of it he was the big arranger uh he's a

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: sometimes the old drummer Chauncey Morehouse from the Jean Goldkette band was there and in fact one day I came about 15 minutes early and Chauncey was there and Bill and they were playing four hands at the piano
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: while I never met Bix, I hung out with Joe Venuti, I hung out with Doc Ryker who was the lead alto saxophone, Chauncey Morehouse the drummer was still alive
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Vaché interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Georg Brunis
about_quotes.evidence: with Chauncey Morehouse, Charlie Spivak, Jimmy Lytell, Claude Thornhill, who made them on the Varsity record.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Well, he's about the only one I remember in that band. Because I didn't know too many people in that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: some of that old stuff — which is wonderful stuff with Frankie Trumbauer and Chauncey Morehouse on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015
